title: Wits Law APS Requirements 2026 | LLB Admission Guide
metaTitle: Wits Law APS Requirements 2026 | LLB Admission Guide
metaDescription: Wits Law (LLB) requires APS 38+ in 2026. English 5+, Mathematics 4+ required. Step-by-step application guide, NBT info, and tips to improve your score.
content:
Wits University Law APS Requirements 2026

The University of the Witwatersrand (Wits) School of Law offers one of the most prestigious LLB (Bachelor of Laws) programmes in South Africa. Admission is competitive, and meeting the APS requirements is the first step. This guide covers the APS and subject requirements for Wits Law in 2026.

Use our free APS Calculator to check your eligibility instantly.

Wits LLB Admission Requirements at a Glance

RequirementDetails
Minimum APS38 (out of 42, excluding Life Orientation)
English Home LanguageLevel 5 (60%+)
English First Additional LanguageLevel 6 (70%+)
MathematicsLevel 4 (50%+) OR Mathematical Literacy Level 6 (70%+)
NBT RequiredYes — Academic Literacy (AL) test
Programme Duration4 years (LLB)
Application DeadlineSeptember 30
Application FeeR100

Understanding Wits Law APS

Wits calculates APS from your six best NSC subjects, excluding Life Orientation. For Law, you need an APS of 38 or higher. This is a high requirement — it means most of your six subjects need to be in the 60%+ range.

Worked example for Wits Law APS 38:

SubjectMark NeededAPS Points
English Home Language75% (Level 6)6
Mathematics65% (Level 5)5
History80% (Level 7)7
Geography80% (Level 7)7
Accounting75% (Level 6)6
Afrikaans FAL80% (Level 7)7
Wits APS Total (excl. LO)38 — meets requirement

To achieve APS 38, you need consistently strong marks across all six subjects. Most Law applicants who are accepted have APS scores well above the minimum.

NBT Requirement for Wits Law

Wits Law requires the NBT Academic Literacy (AL) test. NBT results are used alongside your NSC marks to assess your academic readiness. Register and write the NBT at www.nbt.ac.za. Write early — NBT test seats fill up, especially in major centres.

Wits LLB Programme Structure

The Wits LLB is a 4-year degree that qualifies graduates to practice as attorneys, advocates, or work in other legal fields. Year 1 covers foundational law; Years 2–4 cover specialisations including contract, criminal, constitutional, commercial, and procedural law.

After completing the LLB, graduates must complete:

  • Attorney: 2-year articles of clerkship + board exams (LSSA)
  • Advocate: Pupillage at a Bar + call to the bar

How Competitive is Wits Law?

Wits Law is one of the most competitive LLB programmes in South Africa:

  • Wits Law School accepts approximately 300–350 new LLB students per year
  • Thousands of applications are received for each cohort
  • Many successful applicants have APS scores of 40+ with strong NBT results
  • Meeting the minimum APS of 38 does NOT guarantee admission

Comparing Wits Law with Other Law Schools in SA

UniversityMin APS for LLBLLB Duration
Wits384 years
UCT34+ (UCT composite)4 years
University of Pretoria (UP)304 years
Stellenbosch University344 years
UKZN304 years
University of Johannesburg (UJ)284 years
NWU (Potchefstroom)304 years
University of the Free State284 years
UNISA (distance learning)244 years (part-time available)

How to Apply for Wits Law 2026

  1. Calculate your APS: Use our free APS Calculator to confirm you have 38+
  2. Write NBT: Register at www.nbt.ac.za and complete the Academic Literacy test
  3. Apply online: Visit wits.ac.za/students/undergraduate/applying
  4. Application fee: R100
  5. Deadline: September 30

Need to boost your marks to reach APS 38? StudyBuddy's AI tutor helps Grade 10–12 students improve in all subjects. Start free — no credit card required.
keywords: how to apply for law at Wits
quickAnswer: Wits Law (LLB) requires a minimum APS of 38 in 2026, with English at Level 5 (60%+) and Mathematics at Level 4 (50%+) or Mathematical Literacy at Level 6 (70%+). NBT Academic Literacy test is required. Apply by September 30 at wits.ac.za.

faqs.question: What APS do you need for Law at Wits in 2026?
faqs.answer: Wits Law (LLB) requires a minimum APS of 38 (excluding Life Orientation). You also need English at Level 5 (60%+) and Mathematics at Level 4 (50%+) or Mathematical Literacy at Level 6 (70%+). The NBT Academic Literacy test is also required.

faqs.question: Can I do law at Wits with Mathematical Literacy?
faqs.answer: Yes, Wits Law accepts Mathematical Literacy, but at a higher level than pure Mathematics — you need Mathematical Literacy at Level 6 (70%+). Pure Mathematics is only required at Level 4 (50%+). However, your overall APS still needs to be 38+.

faqs.question: How competitive is Wits Law?
faqs.answer: Wits Law is very competitive. While the minimum APS is 38, most admitted students have APS scores of 40 or higher with strong NBT results. Thousands of students apply for a limited number of places each year.

faqs.question: What can you do with a Wits LLB degree?
faqs.answer: A Wits LLB qualifies you to pursue admission as an attorney (after articles) or advocate (after pupillage). Graduates also work in corporate law, government, academia, international organisations, human rights law, and more. Wits Law is highly regarded by top law firms.
